title: A/B Test Setup
description: Expertise in experimentation and A/B testing to design tests that produce statistically valid, actionable results.
core_principles: Start with a Hypothesis: Make specific predictions of outcomes based on reasoning or data.
Test One Thing: Only change a single variable per test to identify exactly what caused the result.
Statistical Rigor: Pre-determine sample sizes and commit to the methodology without 'peeking' early.
Measure What Matters: Tie primary metrics to business value and use guardrail metrics to prevent harm.

title: Ad Creative
description: Expertise in generating, iterating, and scaling ad creative—headlines, descriptions, and visuals—for any paid advertising platform.
core_principles: Define Your Angles: Establish distinct reasons to click, such as pain points, outcomes, or social proof.
Headlines That Click: Focus on specificity, benefits, and an active voice to capture attention.
Descriptions That Convert: Complement headlines with concrete proof points and proactive objection handling.
Respect Constraints: Strictly follow platform-specific character limits to avoid message truncation.

title: AI SEO
description: Expertise in AI search optimization—making content discoverable, extractable, and citable by systems like ChatGPT, Gemini, and Perplexity.
core_principles: Focus on Citations: Traditional SEO aims for ranking; AI SEO aims to be cited as a trusted source.
Three Pillars: Focus on Structure (extractable), Authority (trustworthy data), and Presence (being where AI looks).
Data-Driven Visibility: Including specific statistics and citing sources can boost AI visibility by up to 40%.
Structured Data: Use Schema (FAQ, Product, Article) to help AI systems correctly parse your information.

title: Analytics Tracking
description: Expertise in analytics implementation and measurement, setting up tracking that provides actionable insights for marketing and product decisions.
core_principles: Track for Decisions: Every tracked event should inform a specific decision; prioritize quality over quantity.
Start with Questions: Identify what you need to know first, then work backwards to determine what to track.
Consistent Naming: Use clear Object-Action conventions and document your tracking plan meticulously.
Maintain Data Quality: Regularly validate implementations to monitor for potential data drift or breakage.

title: Churn Prevention
description: Expertise in SaaS retention and reducing both voluntary (cancellations) and involuntary (payment failures) churn.
core_principles: Match Offer to Reason: Personalize the 'save' offer based on the specific reason a customer is leaving.
Structured Cancel Flow: Use a multi-step flow including a survey, dynamic offer, and clear confirmation.
Recover Involuntary Churn: Implement smart payment retries, dunning emails, and card updater services.
Proactive Interventions: Use health scores to identify and assist 'at-risk' customers before they decide to cancel.

title: Cold Email Writing
description: Write B2B cold emails and follow-up sequences that sound like they come from a sharp, thoughtful, and human peer.
core_principles: Peer-to-Peer Voice: Write like a colleague offering value rather than a vendor aggressively selling.
Ruthless Brevity: Every sentence must earn its place; lead with the reader's world, not your own.
Interest-Based CTAs: Use low-friction asks (e.g., 'worth exploring?') instead of hard meeting requests.
Personalized Connections: Ensure personalization naturally transitions into the specific problem you solve.

title: Competitor & Alternative Pages
description: Create competitor comparison or alternative pages to capture high-intent search traffic and position your product effectively.
core_principles: Build Trust with Honesty: Acknowledge competitor strengths accurately to build long-term credibility.
Values-Led Comparison: Focus on why differences matter to the user rather than just checking feature boxes.
Help Them Decide: Clearly state who your product is for and who the competitor might be better for.
Scalable Structure: Use a modular approach to keep competitor information updated across all comparison pages.

title: Content Strategy
description: Plan content that drives traffic, builds authority, and generates leads by balancing searchability and shareability.
core_principles: Searchable vs. Shareable: Balance content that captures existing demand with content that creates new demand.
Lifecycle Mapping: Align content formats and keywords with every stage of the buyer's journey.
Content Clusters: Own 3-5 core pillars and build authority through interlinked, deep-dive content.
Research-Led Insight: Use customer calls, forums, and keyword data to guide your topic ideation.

title: Copy Editing
description: Systematically improve existing marketing copy through focused editing passes without losing the core message or author's voice.
core_principles: The Seven Sweeps: Systematically review for Clarity, Voice, 'So What', Proof, Specificity, Emotion, and Risk.
Preserve the Voice: Enhance the author's existing voice rather than forcing a total stylistic rewrite.
The 'So What' Test: Every claim must answer why the reader should care with a deeper, tangible benefit.
Ruthless Specificity: Replace vague adjectives with concrete numbers, timeframes, and outcomes.

title: Copywriting
description: Expert conversion copywriting for marketing pages (home, landing, pricing) to drive user action and conversions.
core_principles: Clarity Over Cleverness: Always prioritize clear communication over being creative, 'punny', or vague.
Benefits Over Features: Focus on what the product enables for the user, not just its technical specifications.
Specificity Builds Credibility: Use concrete data points (e.g., 'save 12 hours/week') to build trust.
Mirror Customer Language: Use the exact words customers use in reviews and interviews to describe their needs.

title: Email Sequence Design
description: Create automated email flows and lifecycle programs that nurture relationships and drive long-term conversions.
core_principles: One Email, One Job: Every email in a sequence must have exactly one primary goal and one main CTA.
Value Build-Up: Provide genuine utility and helpful resources before ever asking for a sale.
Relevance is King: Segment your audience to ensure every email is highly relevant to the recipient's stage.
Clear momentum: Every email should represent a logical and helpful next step in the user's journey.

title: Form CRO
description: Optimize non-registration forms (lead capture, contact, checkout) to minimize friction and maximize completion rates.
core_principles: Field Minimization: Every extra field has a conversion 'cost'; remove all non-essential requirements.
Value > Effort: Ensure the perceived reward for completing the form is greater than the effort required.
Reduce Cognitive Load: Use single-column layouts, clear labels, and logical field groupings.
Build Commitment: Start with the easiest or most rewarding fields to build user momentum through the form.

title: Free Tool Strategy
description: Use 'Engineering as Marketing' by building free tools that generate leads, build SEO value, and establish authority.
core_principles: Solve Real Problems: The tool must provide genuine standalone value, not just be a marketing gimmick.
Product Adjacency: The tool should naturally lead users toward your core paid product solution.
Immediate Gratification: Provide value instantly with low friction, avoiding massive upfront signups.
Strategic Investment: Evaluate tools based on projected lead value versus the cost of build and maintenance.

title: Launch Strategy
description: Plan SaaS product and feature launches that build sustainable momentum and convert interest into active users.
core_principles: The ORB Framework: Leverage Owned (email), Rented (social), and Borrowed (partners) channels in coordination.
Momentum Phases: Use Alpha, Beta, and Early Access phases to build anticipation before a full public launch.
Iterative Launches: Treat every significant update or new feature as a fresh opportunity to capture attention.
Drive to Owned: Always aim to convert temporary social attention into permanent email relationships.

title: Lead Magnets
description: Create high-value resources that capture email addresses and solve specific audience pain points effectively.
core_principles: Ultra-Specific Solution: Address one clear, painful problem rather than covering a broad, generic topic.
High Perceived Value: The content should feel valuable enough that users would consider paying for it.
Quick Consumability: Aim for value that can be fully realized by the user in under 10-30 minutes.
Product Education: Show the gap that exists in their current process and how your product bridges it.

title: Marketing Ideas for SaaS
description: Find and implement the right marketing strategies for different business situations, stages, and resource constraints.
core_principles: Contextual Appropriateness: Evaluate every marketing tactic based on your specific audience and stage.
Multi-Channel Approach: Coordinate efforts across Owned, Rented, and Borrowed channels for maximum reach.
Success-Oriented Phasing: Match implementation speed and budget to the expected business outcomes.
Actionable Outputs: Ensure every idea translates into a concrete, measurable, and documented next step.

title: Marketing Psychology & Mental Models
description: Apply psychological principles and mental models to understand and influence customer behavior ethically and effectively.
core_principles: Jobs to Be Done: Focus on the specific outcome or 'job' customers hire your product to achieve.
First Principles Thinking: Break problems down to fundamental truths instead of simply copying competitor trends.
Loss Aversion: Leverage the fact that people tend to value avoiding a loss more than achieving an equivalent gain.
Social Proof: Use customer counts, testimonials, and case studies to signal quality and reduce perceived risk.

title: Onboarding CRO
description: Optimize user activation and the first-run experience to help users reach their 'aha moment' as fast as possible.
core_principles: Minimize Time-to-Value: Eliminate every friction point between signup and the user's first success.
Goal-Oriented Sessions: Focus the very first session on achieving a single, meaningful successful outcome.
Active Learning: Use interactive 'doing' walkthroughs instead of passive 'showing' tutorials.
Progress Momentum: Use progress bars and celebration moments to keep users motivated and moving forward.

title: Page Conversion Rate Optimization (CRO)
description: Analyze and optimize marketing pages to improve the percentage of visitors who take a desired action.
core_principles: 5-Second Clarity: Ensure users understand the value proposition and the next step within 5 seconds of landing.
Strategic Trust Building: Place social proof and trust signals (logos, reviews) near key decision points.
Proactive Objection Handling: Address common concerns like price, security, and risk directly in the copy.
Friction Removal: Simplify navigation and forms to make the path to conversion as obvious as possible.

title: Paid Ads
description: Create, optimize, and scale paid advertising campaigns that drive efficient customer acquisition across top platforms.
core_principles: Strategic Platform Choice: Use search ads for existing intent and social ads for demand generation.
Account Hierarchy: Maintain a clear structure (Campaign > Ad Set > Ad) with consistent naming conventions.
Testing-Scaling Balance: Use a 70/30 split between proven winning campaigns and experimental testing.
Funnel-Based Retargeting: Personalize retargeting messages based on previous high-intent user interactions.
